patterns.name: AI Safety and Governance Separation Across Planes
patterns.summary: Separate AI policy and governance (Control) from workflow execution (Application) and inference/compute (Data).

patterns.name: Agent Identity Pattern (Normative Summary)
patterns.summary: Treat agents as first-class principals with explicit identity, attribution, and policy-governed permissions.

patterns.name: Channel Constraints Do Not Change Plane Authority
patterns.summary: Treat channels as delivery constraints; do not use channel limitations to justify control-plane authority leakage.

patterns.name: Evidence Generation & Traceability
patterns.summary: Compliance evidence MUST be a **byproduct of normal system operation**.

patterns.name: Failure Modes & Anti-Patterns
patterns.summary: The following are **architectural defects**:

patterns.name: Purpose & Role in the Architecture Library
patterns.summary: This document defines the **Contura Data Governance & Data Quality Standards v1**.

patterns.name: Why Data Governance Must Be Architectural
patterns.summary: In Contura systems, data governance is not a policy overlay or organizational process.

patterns.name: Data as a First-Class Governed Asset
patterns.summary: All data in Contura systems MUST be treated as a **first-class governed asset**.

patterns.name: Data Governance Responsibilities Across the Tri-Plane Architecture
patterns.summary: Data governance responsibilities are intentionally separated across the **Control Plane**, **Application Plane**, and **Data Plane**.

patterns.name: Data Classification and Sensitivity Domains
patterns.summary: All data assets MUST be classified.

patterns.name: Data Quality Dimensions and Invariants
patterns.summary: Data quality is a **continuous, machine-evaluable property**.

patterns.name: Data Lifecycle Governance (Creation to Deletion)
patterns.summary: All data MUST have an explicit lifecycle.

patterns.name: Design-Time Data Governance (ADR Integration)
patterns.summary: All Architectural Decision Records (ADRs) that affect data MUST:

patterns.name: Runtime Data Validation and Enforcement
patterns.summary: Runtime data governance is mandatory.

patterns.name: AI and Agent Data Usage Constraints
patterns.summary: AI agents amplify data governance risks.

patterns.name: Integration with Policy Engine
patterns.summary: Data governance rules MUST be encoded as **policy**.

patterns.name: Integration with Compliance Automation
patterns.summary: Data governance is a **compliance domain**.

patterns.name: Data Observability, Lineage, and Trust Signals
patterns.summary: All governed data MUST emit observability signals.

patterns.name: Failure Modes & Anti-Patterns
patterns.summary: The following are architectural defects:

patterns.name: Evolution, Migration, and Backward Compatibility
patterns.summary: Data governance rules MUST evolve intentionally.

patterns.name: Version History
patterns.summary: - **v1** — Initial release defining data governance and data quality as first-class, enforceable architectural standards across Contura systems.

patterns.name: Backend-for-Frontend (BFF) / API Composition Boundary
patterns.summary: Introduce a UI-facing boundary that provides a client-optimized, synchronous interface while bridging to asynchronous and/or multi-backend interactions behind the boundary.

patterns.name: Contura Enforcement Chain
patterns.summary: Plain-text flow:

patterns.name: Artifact Types and Roles
patterns.summary: Derived from architecture_library/60_executable_architecture_overview_v1.md section 'Artifact Types and Roles'.

patterns.name: Mapping Requirements to Checks
patterns.summary: To be executable, normative requirements must be mapped to stable check IDs.

patterns.name: Non-Goals and Anti-Patterns
patterns.summary: The executable layer must not become:

patterns.name: Identity Principal Taxonomy (Platform/Tenant/Service/Agent)
patterns.summary: Define canonical principal types and treat identity as a platform concern with explicit governance.

patterns.name: Identity and Context Propagation
patterns.summary: Propagate principal identity and required context deterministically across boundaries and planes.

patterns.name: Identity Auditability and Traceability
patterns.summary: Make identity decisions and enforcement traceable via audit logs and telemetry that include principal attribution.

patterns.name: Application Plane Authentication & Authorization Patterns
patterns.summary: The Application Plane is responsible for **authentication validation** and **authorization enforcement** within the boundaries defined by Control Plane identity governance.

patterns.name: Data Plane Access Enforcement Patterns
patterns.summary: The Data Plane is responsible for **access enforcement at the data layer**, based on identity and context established upstream.

patterns.name: Identity and Context Propagation
patterns.summary: Identity and access enforcement across planes depends on the **reliable propagation of identity and execution context**.

patterns.name: Policy Delegation and Enforcement Boundaries
patterns.summary: Policy in Contura systems is defined, delegated, and enforced across planes with **explicit boundaries**.

patterns.name: Agent Identity Patterns
patterns.summary: Agents are **first-class principals** that execute autonomous or semi-autonomous workflows. They are governed by the same identity and policy delegation principles as other principals, with additional constraints to p...

patterns.name: Zero Trust Identity Patterns Across Planes
patterns.summary: Contura systems apply **Zero Trust** as a principle of **identity and access**, not as a network topology or product choice.

patterns.name: Observability, Auditability, and Traceability for Identity
patterns.summary: Identity and access controls are only meaningful if they are **observable, auditable, and traceable**.

patterns.name: Prohibited Identity and Access Anti-Patterns
patterns.summary: This section enumerates **identity and access anti-patterns that are explicitly prohibited** in Contura-compliant systems.

patterns.name: ADR Integration and Evolution
patterns.summary: Derived from architecture_library/22_contura_identity_access_pattern_guide_v1.md section 'ADR Integration and Evolution'.

patterns.name: Authentication Validation
patterns.summary: Application Plane systems validate incoming identities according to Control Plane–governed integration and trust rules.

patterns.name: Delegated Authorization Enforcement
patterns.summary: Application Plane systems enforce authorization decisions using **delegated policy intent**.

patterns.name: Tenant-Context–Aware Authorization
patterns.summary: All authorization in the Application Plane is evaluated in explicit tenant context.

patterns.name: Runtime Policy Consumption
patterns.summary: Application Plane systems consume policy intent at runtime without becoming policy authorities.

patterns.name: Agent and Service Authorization
patterns.summary: Application Plane systems enforce authorization for services and agents using the same governance boundaries as for users.

patterns.name: Failure Handling and Denial
patterns.summary: Authorization failures are handled explicitly and consistently.

patterns.name: Centralized Identity Authority
patterns.summary: All platform-wide identity authority is centralized in the Control Plane.

patterns.name: Tenant-Scoped Identity Governance
patterns.summary: The Control Plane governs identity **within explicit tenant boundaries**.

patterns.name: Identity Integration Governance
patterns.summary: The Control Plane governs how external identity providers integrate with the platform.

patterns.name: Policy Intent Definition
patterns.summary: The Control Plane defines **identity-related policy intent**, which downstream systems enforce.

patterns.name: Identity Lifecycle Governance
patterns.summary: The Control Plane governs identity lifecycle transitions.

patterns.name: Delegated Enforcement Boundary
patterns.summary: The Control Plane explicitly defines **delegation boundaries** for identity enforcement.

patterns.name: Identity Attribution
patterns.summary: Every significant action must be attributable to a governed identity.

patterns.name: End-to-End Traceability
patterns.summary: Identity context must be traceable end-to-end across planes and workflows.

patterns.name: Revocation and Change Propagation
patterns.summary: Policy delegation is revocable and subject to change.

patterns.name: No Transitive Authority
patterns.summary: Delegated enforcement authority is not transitive.

patterns.name: Policy Signals vs Control Signals
patterns.summary: Policy evaluation outcomes are not control intent.

patterns.name: No Implicit Trust Between Planes
patterns.summary: No plane implicitly trusts another plane.

patterns.name: Explicit Identity Verification
patterns.summary: Every access path requires explicit identity verification.

patterns.name: Severity Levels (Operational)
patterns.summary: - **SEV-0 (Critical):** Confirmed cross-tenant data exposure or cross-tenant action. - **SEV-1 (High):** Strong indicator of imminent exposure/action; containment required. - **SEV-2 (Medium):** Tenancy control gap wi...

patterns.name: Post-Incident Requirements
patterns.summary: For any SEV-0 / SEV-1: - A Tenancy Readiness ADR update is required - Add at least one automated test that fails without the fix - Add monitoring/alerts tied to tenant_id and principal

patterns.name: Domain Modeling Framework Selection (DDD + Clean Architecture Default)
patterns.summary: Select a modeling framework that yields plane-aware boundaries, invariants, and test seams without prescribing domain-specific nouns.

patterns.name: MRAD-1 Idempotent Command Pattern
patterns.summary: Mobile-triggered state changes are retry-safe; duplicate submissions do not create duplicate effects.

patterns.name: MRAD-2 Resumable Workflow Pattern
patterns.summary: Long-running actions return durable identifiers and can be resumed/observed without restarting.

patterns.name: MRAD-3 Bounded Payload and Collection Pattern
patterns.summary: Mobile-facing APIs provide bounded response shapes via pagination and controlled expansions.

patterns.name: MRAD-4 Coarse-Grained Interaction Pattern
patterns.summary: Avoid chatty sequences on critical paths by using coarse-grained operations and server-side aggregation.

patterns.name: MRAD-5 Offline/Degraded Mode Pattern
patterns.summary: Where meaningful, support offline/degraded behavior such as draft capture and later synchronization.

patterns.name: Background Execution Constraint
patterns.summary: Mobile clients must not be required to remain active for workflow completion.

patterns.name: Multi-Tenancy as First-Class Architectural Concern
patterns.summary: Make tenancy explicit, enforce invariants, and plan for enterprise/compliance evolution without redesign.

patterns.summary: Agents amplify observability requirements.

patterns.name: Audit Trails as First-Class Artifacts

patterns.summary: Metering MUST be treated as a mandatory execution step, not a side effect.

patterns.summary: Diagrams are ASCII/plain-text only. Diagrams add explanatory value (not decorative). Diagrams reflect SaaS and tri-plane realities. Diagrams do not contradict textual descriptions.

patterns.name: Domain / Subdomain-Based Routing
patterns.summary: Bind tenant identity via DNS or host-level routing.

patterns.name: Token-Embedded Tenant Claims
patterns.summary: Bind tenant identity via cryptographically signed credentials.

patterns.name: Explicit Tenant Selection with Session Binding
patterns.summary: Allow users who belong to multiple tenants to explicitly select context.

patterns.name: Pooled Everything (Logical Isolation Baseline)
patterns.summary: Maximize operational efficiency by serving all tenants from shared infrastructure, enforcing isolation via tenant-aware software and policy.

patterns.name: Pooled Compute + Partitioned Data (Bridge Isolation)
patterns.summary: **Pooled Compute + Partitioned Data: Intent** Retain shared application infrastructure while increasing isolation and lifecycle flexibility by partitioning tenant data.

patterns.name: Silo Tenants (Dedicated Infrastructure)
patterns.summary: **Silo Tenants: Intent** Provide maximal isolation by giving one tenant dedicated infrastructure boundaries.

patterns.name: Hybrid Isolation (Selective and Tiered)
patterns.summary: Allow a SaaS platform to start pooled, then selectively apply stronger isolation where justified—without redesign.

patterns.summary: Tenant provisioning establishes a new tenant and its initial governance state.

patterns.summary: Observability enables **blast radius detection**.

patterns.name: 1. Global Tenancy Invariants

patterns.name: 10. Anti-Pattern Detection Checklist
patterns.summary: Is any tenant context implicit or inferred? Are workspaces, projects, or folders acting as tenants? Are billing plans or cost tiers used for authorization or isolation? Is any identity operating without tenant scope?...

patterns.name: Global Tenancy Invariants
patterns.summary: **Does every Resource belong to exactly one Tenant?**

patterns.name: Routing & Tenant Context Validation
patterns.summary: **Is tenant identity resolved exactly once per request or invocation?**

patterns.name: Identity & Access Validation
patterns.summary: **Is every identity action evaluated within explicit Tenant Context?**

patterns.name: Lifecycle Validation
patterns.summary: **Is tenant creation deliberate and high-friction?**

patterns.name: Isolation Validation
patterns.summary: **Is the selected isolation mode explicit metadata governed by Control Plane policy?**

patterns.name: Cost & Usage Enforcement Validation
patterns.summary: **Is usage metered at well-defined execution boundaries?**

patterns.name: Observability, Audit & Safety Validation
patterns.summary: **Do all logs include tenant identifiers?**

patterns.name: Agent-Specific Validation
patterns.summary: **Do agents always operate under explicit Tenant Context?**

patterns.name: Enterprise & Compliance Mode Validation
patterns.summary: **Are compliance modes explicit, named, and policy-governed?**

patterns.name: Anti-Pattern Detection Checklist
patterns.summary: **Is any tenant context implicit or inferred?**

patterns.name: Tri-Plane Separation (Control/Application/Data)
patterns.summary: Define Control Plane, Application Plane, and Data Plane as distinct architectural roles with explicit boundaries.

patterns.name: Plane Runtime Shape Declaration
patterns.summary: Declare each plane's runtime shape and ingress mode (HTTP, events, embedded library) so the derivation cascade can compile deployable responsibilities without framework assumptions.

patterns.name: Policy as a First-Class System Artifact
patterns.summary: Represent policy as machine-evaluable, versioned, auditable artifacts rather than ad-hoc checks or configuration.

patterns.name: Policy Responsibilities Across Planes
patterns.summary: Separate policy truth and lifecycle (Control) from evaluation/enforcement (Application) and access enforcement (Data).

patterns.name: Policy Decision Telemetry and Audit
patterns.summary: Require observability for policy evaluation, including decision lineage, inputs attribution, and outcomes.

patterns.name: Policy Types and Domains
patterns.summary: Policy is classified by **domain**, not by regulation or feature.

patterns.name: Policy Lifecycle and Versioning
patterns.summary: Policy lifecycle is explicit and controlled.

patterns.name: Design-Time Policy (ADR Integration)
patterns.summary: Policies originate from **architectural intent**.

patterns.name: Runtime Policy Evaluation
patterns.summary: Policy evaluation is a **deterministic system operation**.

patterns.name: Policy Enforcement Patterns
patterns.summary: Policy enforcement is not optional.

patterns.name: Integration with AI Safety Gates
patterns.summary: AI Safety Gates are **policy enforcement points**.

patterns.name: Integration with Compliance Automation
patterns.summary: Compliance is policy enforced continuously.

patterns.name: Integration with Cost Governance
patterns.summary: Cost is governed by policy.

patterns.name: Policy Observability, Evidence, and Drift
patterns.summary: Every policy decision MUST produce evidence.

patterns.name: Failure Modes & Anti-Patterns
patterns.summary: The following are architectural defects:

patterns.name: Evolution and Backward Compatibility
patterns.summary: Policy evolution MUST be intentional.

patterns.name: Resource Identifier Strategy (Opaque vs Natural Key)
patterns.summary: Select a stable identifier strategy for tenant-scoped resources and define addressing semantics.

patterns.name: Derivation Contract for Phase 7
patterns.summary: All Phase 7 reference implementations MUST satisfy the following rules.

patterns.name: Canonical Phase 7 Directory Structure
patterns.summary: Each reference implementation MUST conform to the following directory structure.

patterns.name: Template Declaration Requirement
patterns.summary: Each reference implementation MUST explicitly declare:

patterns.name: Explicit Parameter Pinning
patterns.summary: All parameters exposed by each instantiated template MUST be:

patterns.name: No Architectural Invention
patterns.summary: Reference implementations:

patterns.name: Application Plane Responsibilities (Safety & Guardrails)
patterns.summary: - Integrate Safety Gate checks into AI workflows. - Respect Safety Gate decisions (block, redact, defer to human). - Surface meaningful error / explanation messages to users.

patterns.name: Control Plane Responsibilities (Safety & Guardrails)
patterns.summary: - Define Safety Gate configurations (thresholds, escalation rules). - Register which AI flows require human review. - Ensure Safety Gates are applied consistently across all AP services.

patterns.name: Data Plane Responsibilities (Safety & Guardrails)
patterns.summary: - Store Safety Gate logs where needed. - Ensure that AI-related data (e.g., prompts, outputs) are retained or deleted per policy.

patterns.name: Tenant Context Propagation (Normative)
patterns.summary: Treat tenant as a first-class concept and require explicit tenant context propagation across planes.

patterns.name: Application Plane Telemetry
patterns.summary: - Per-tenant request rates, latencies, errors - AI agent traces (tools called, steps taken) - Authorization failures - Feature usage metrics and workflow outcomes

patterns.name: Control Plane Telemetry
patterns.summary: - Tenant lifecycle events - Policy changes and configuration history - AI governance decisions (e.g., model version assignments) - Operator and admin actions

patterns.name: Data Plane Telemetry
patterns.summary: - Query performance and errors - Storage utilization per tenant / shard - Model inference metrics (latency, failures, cost) - Data policy execution (retention, deletion)

patterns.name: Versioning Expectations
patterns.summary: - Major changes to responsibilities or boundaries **MUST** be recorded as ADRs. - AI model and prompt versions **MUST** be governed via the Control Plane. - Per-plane deployments **SHOULD** be independently versioned.

patterns.name: Application Plane: Tenant Workflow Orchestration + Internal Choreography
patterns.summary: - Application Plane orchestrates **tenant business workflows** (e.g., purchase flows, focus space workflows) using CP policy and DP data. - AP may use **choreography** between its own services (e.g., event-driven micr...

patterns.name: Control Plane: Orchestration Only
patterns.summary: - Control Plane acts as a **central orchestrator** of platform workflows: - Tenant provisioning and offboarding - Resource scaling and migration - Policy rollout and rollback - CP may use **events or message buses** t...

patterns.name: Data Plane: No Orchestration of Governance
patterns.summary: - Data Plane may orchestrate **data-internal** tasks (e.g., index maintenance), but **not** tenant lifecycle or platform policies.

patterns.name: Allowed Cross-Plane Interaction Patterns
patterns.summary: Define the approved shapes for Control↔Application↔Data plane interactions and delegation.

patterns.name: Prohibited Cross-Plane Interaction Anti-Patterns
patterns.summary: Enumerate forbidden cross-plane interactions that violate authority, auditability, or safety.

patterns.name: Zero Trust Norms Across Planes
patterns.summary: Assume no trusted internal network; require authentication/authorization and explicit context for every operation.

patterns.name: Configuration Boundary
patterns.summary: Centralize configuration definition, loading, and validation behind a configuration boundary so runtime settings are explicit, validated, and injectable.

patterns.name: Composition Root and Layered Boundaries
patterns.summary: Centralize application assembly in a single composition root and enforce one-way dependency direction across named layers and boundaries.

patterns.name: Request Context and Propagation
patterns.summary: Establish a request-scoped context that carries identity, tenancy, and correlation metadata and ensure it is available to all layers handling the request.

patterns.name: Anti-corruption Layer
patterns.summary: Adapter layer to isolate domain from external systems

patterns.name: API Composition / Aggregator
patterns.summary: One endpoint composes multiple downstream calls

patterns.name: API Gateway
patterns.summary: Central API policy enforcement (auth, quotas, transforms, versioning)

patterns.name: Auditability
patterns.summary: Strong logs + traceability

patterns.name: Backend-for-Frontend (BFF)
patterns.summary: Client-specific API to simplify UI and reduce chatty calls

patterns.name: Backup & PITR
patterns.summary: Restore to a point in time

patterns.name: Blue/Green Deploy
patterns.summary: Two environments, switch traffic atomically

patterns.name: Bulkhead Isolation
patterns.summary: Separate pools/resources per workload/tenant

patterns.name: Cache-Aside
patterns.summary: App-managed caching strategy

patterns.name: Canary Release
patterns.summary: Gradual traffic shift to new version

patterns.name: Choreography
patterns.summary: Services coordinate via events (no central controller)

patterns.name: Chunking + Metadata Enrichment
patterns.summary: Normalize chunks + provenance + ACL tags

patterns.name: Circuit Breaker
patterns.summary: Stop calling a failing dependency to reduce blast radius

patterns.name: Competing Consumers
patterns.summary: Many workers pull from same queue to scale

patterns.name: Config Externalization
patterns.summary: Keep config outside binaries

patterns.name: Cost Allocation / Chargeback
patterns.summary: Tagging + budgets + showback

patterns.name: CQRS
patterns.summary: Separate the read model from the write model

patterns.name: Dead Letter Queue (DLQ)
patterns.summary: Isolate poison messages for triage/replay

patterns.name: Defense in Depth
patterns.summary: Layered security controls

patterns.name: Distributed Tracing
patterns.summary: Correlate calls across services

patterns.name: Edge Gateway
patterns.summary: Single global entry for TLS/WAF/DDoS & routing

patterns.name: Event-Driven Architecture
patterns.summary: React to events instead of synchronous chaining

patterns.name: Feature Flags
patterns.summary: Toggle behavior safely

patterns.name: Golden Path Templates
patterns.summary: Opinionated starter kits

patterns.name: Graceful Degradation
patterns.summary: Reduce features instead of failing hard

patterns.name: Guardrails / Safety Filters
patterns.summary: Control unsafe output & injection

patterns.name: Hybrid Retrieval
patterns.summary: Combine vector + keyword (+ semantic ranking)

patterns.name: Idempotency
patterns.summary: Safe retries without duplicate side effects

patterns.name: Immutable Infrastructure
patterns.summary: Rebuild artifacts instead of patching in place

patterns.name: Kubernetes Platform
patterns.summary: Full Kubernetes for complex platform needs

patterns.name: Landing Zone
patterns.summary: Standard org/accounts, network, logging, security baseline

patterns.name: Least Privilege (RBAC/IAM)
patterns.summary: Minimal permissions per principal

patterns.name: Managed Containers
patterns.summary: Run containers w/ minimal ops (no K8s management)

patterns.name: Materialized View
patterns.summary: Precomputed read projection for speed

patterns.name: Multi-Env Promotion
patterns.summary: Dev→Test→Prod via pipelines and IaC

patterns.name: Multi-Region Active/Active
patterns.summary: Serve traffic in multiple regions

patterns.name: Multi-Region Active/Passive
patterns.summary: DR standby

patterns.name: Observability “3 pillars”
patterns.summary: Metrics + logs + traces as default

patterns.name: Orchestration
patterns.summary: The central coordinator controls the workflow

patterns.name: Outbox Pattern
patterns.summary: Atomic DB write + event publish reliability

patterns.name: Policy-as-Code / Guardrails
patterns.summary: Prevent insecure deployments

patterns.name: Polyglot Persistence
patterns.summary: Use best datastore per use case

patterns.name: Private Connectivity
patterns.summary: Keep PaaS/services private

patterns.name: Prompt Versioning
patterns.summary: Prompts as artifacts w/ rollout & rollback

patterns.name: Pub/Sub
patterns.summary: Fan-out events to multiple subscribers

patterns.name: Queue-based Load Leveling
patterns.summary: Buffer spikes; protect dependencies

patterns.name: RAG
patterns.summary: Retrieval-grounded generation w/ citations

patterns.name: Rate Limiting / Quotas
patterns.summary: Prevent abuse and runaway costs

patterns.name: Read-Through Cache
patterns.summary: Cache fetches on miss (library/pattern)

patterns.name: Retry w/ Backoff + Jitter
patterns.summary: Controlled retries for transient failures

patterns.name: Saga
patterns.summary: Distributed transaction with compensations

patterns.name: Secrets Management
patterns.summary: Central vault, rotation, no secrets in code

patterns.name: Security Trimming
patterns.summary: Filter results by user permissions

patterns.name: Serverless
patterns.summary: Event-driven compute for glue and bursty workloads

patterns.name: Sidecar
patterns.summary: Add cross-cutting features via companion container

patterns.name: SLO/SLI Monitoring
patterns.summary: Alert on user-impacting metrics

patterns.name: Stateless Service
patterns.summary: Scale horizontally; no server-side session

patterns.name: Strangler Fig
patterns.summary: Incrementally replace legacy behind a gateway

patterns.name: Structured Logging
patterns.summary: Queryable logs with a consistent schema

patterns.name: Timeout + Deadlines
patterns.summary: Bound latency; propagate deadlines downstream

patterns.name: Write-Through Cache
patterns.summary: Cache updated on writes

patterns.name: Zero Trust
patterns.summary: Verify identity/context; minimize trust in network

patterns.name: External Integration Adapter Boundary
patterns.summary: Isolate external dependencies behind internal ports and dedicated adapters so the core remains stable and testable.

patterns.name: Observability Boundary
patterns.summary: Ensure consistent telemetry (logs, metrics, traces identifiers) is emitted for requests, key operations, and failures using a stable observability boundary and correlation from request context.

patterns.name: Policy Enforcement Boundary
patterns.summary: Centralize authorization and policy enforcement at defined entry points so protected operations cannot be invoked without a policy decision.

patterns.name: Persistence Boundary via Repositories
patterns.summary: Encapsulate all persistence access behind repository ports so domain and application services remain independent of storage technology.

patterns.name: Application Service Facade Boundary
patterns.summary: Provide a stable application boundary that exposes resource and use-case operations and orchestrates domain logic, persistence, and policies behind a consistent interface.

patterns.name: Validation and Error Handling Boundary
patterns.summary: Validate inputs at boundaries and map failures into stable error contracts so invalid data and unhandled exceptions do not leak into or out of the core.
